title: plectranthus parviflorus var. australis
intro: Plectranthus parviflorus var. australis, commonly known as Mountain Balm or Austral Spinach, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is native to Australia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, and is believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can provide relief from stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, Plectranthus parviflorus var. australis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal benefits.
